deep_dive.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
deep_dive.abstract: Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: builds knowledge graphs from scientific papers using LLMs, enabling automated knowledge discovery and orchestration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大模型从科学论文中构建知识图谱，实现自动化知识发现与编排
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用大模型从科学论文中构建知识图谱，实现自动化知识发现与编排
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用大模型从科学论文中构建知识图谱，实现自动化知识发现与编排
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Agents-K1, an end-to-end pipeline that converts raw scientific documents into agent-native knowledge graphs using a multimodal parser and a 4B model, enabling fine-grained scientific knowledge discovery.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对现有研究智能体忽视科学知识编排的问题，提出Agents-K1。该流水线将原始文档转化为智能体原生知识图谱，包含多模态解析器以捕获实体、跨模态证据、引用等。为科学推理提供结构化知识，促进知识发现。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对现有研究智能体忽视科学知识编排的问题，提出Agents-K1。该流水线将原始文档转化为智能体原生知识图谱，包含多模态解析器以捕获实体、跨模态证据、引用等。为科学推理提供结构化知识，促进知识发现。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 我希望去找一些自动发现算法相关的论文，用大模型启发式计算的那种, especially work that connects or combines: algorithms for automatic discovery in data and knowledge; using large language models for discovery tasks; heuristic methods in computational discovery; knowledge discovery with LLM and heuristics; discovery of algorithms using neural networks; symbolic regression with heuristic search; how can large language models be used for automatic discovery of mathematical formulas; heuristic search guided by language models for scientific discovery; automatic discovery algorithms combining LLM and evolutionary computation.

deep_dive.title: PRISMR: Overcoming Parse Collapse in Multimodal Listwise Ranking via Parameterized Representation Internalization
deep_dive.abstract: Generative listwise ranking with Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) aims to capture global list context in a single forward pass, but   its effectiveness degrades in long-context multimodal scenarios. We identify a recurring failure mode, parse collapse, where the   autoregressive decoder produces fluent yet incomplete rankings by silently omitting candidates and terminating early. This   failure stems from limited context utilization rather than simple formatting mistakes, making prompt engineering and constrained   decoding insufficient. We propose PRISMR (Parameterized Representation Internalization for Semantic Multimodal Ranking), a   framework that replaces transient in-context list processing with parametric structural conditioning. PRISMR uses a lightweight   hypernetwork to encode multimodal candidates in parallel and generate item-specific LoRA weights, which are synthesized into an   instance-specific adapter for a LMM. This paradigm enables more robust internalization of list structure while preserving the   base model. We further introduce a large-scale multimodal review-ranking benchmark for evaluation. Experiments demonstrate that   PRISMR substantially reduces parse collapse, improves listwise ranking performance, and transfers effectively across domains and   instruction-tuned backbones.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: addresses parse collapse in generative listwise ranking with LMMs, a key challenge in listwise reranker training
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 解决多模态列表排序中的解析崩溃问题，是列表式重排序器训练的关键挑战
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 解决多模态列表排序中的解析崩溃问题，是列表式重排序器训练的关键挑战
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 解决多模态列表排序中的解析崩溃问题，是列表式重排序器训练的关键挑战
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Identifies parse collapse in multimodal listwise ranking and proposes PRISMR that replaces in-context list processing with parameterized representation internalization to improve ranking completeness and effectiveness.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文发现多模态列表排序中解析崩溃问题：自回归解码器遗漏候选项并提前终止。提出PRISMR框架，用参数化表示内化替代上下文列表处理，有效缓解解析崩溃。实验表明在多模态长上下文场景下排名完整性和效果显著提升。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文发现多模态列表排序中解析崩溃问题：自回归解码器遗漏候选项并提前终止。提出PRISMR框架，用参数化表示内化替代上下文列表处理，有效缓解解析崩溃。实验表明在多模态长上下文场景下排名完整性和效果显著提升。

deep_dive.title: Leveraging Audio-LLMs to Filter Speech-to-Speech Training Data
deep_dive.abstract: Large-scale mined corpora provide abundant training data for end-to-end speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) but may contain noise, misalignment, and semantic errors. Filtering noisy data is crucial to maintain robust speech translation performance. We study how to train an audio-language model to make keep/drop decisions on paired speech directly from audio. To obtain reliable supervision without manual labels, we adopt a scalable two-stage Rank-to-Distill strategy. A lightweight ranker generates keep/drop pseudo-labels from noisy speech pairs, then trains an audio large language model to predict keep/drop directly from raw paired speech. The resulting model jointly captures acoustic fidelity and cross-lingual semantic consistency for the selection of speech-conditioned data. Experiments on CVSS-C and SpeechMatrix show consistent improvements over unfiltered training, yielding up to +1.4 ASR-BLEU for end-to-end S2ST.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Two-stage Rank-to-Distill strategy to train an audio-LLM as a ranker for data filtering
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 两阶段Rank-to-Distill策略训练音频LLM作为数据筛选排序器
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 两阶段Rank-to-Distill策略训练音频LLM作为数据筛选排序器
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 两阶段Rank-to-Distill策略训练音频LLM作为数据筛选排序器
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a scalable two-stage Rank-to-Distill method to train an audio-LLM as a ranker for filtering speech-to-speech training data.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对语音翻译大规模语料库噪声问题，提出两阶段Rank-to-Distill策略，先训练轻量级排序器生成伪标签，再训练音频LLM直接预测保留/丢弃，模型联合捕获声学保真度和跨语言语义一致性，为语音翻译数据筛选提供高效排序训练方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对语音翻译大规模语料库噪声问题，提出两阶段Rank-to-Distill策略，先训练轻量级排序器生成伪标签，再训练音频LLM直接预测保留/丢弃，模型联合捕获声学保真度和跨语言语义一致性，为语音翻译数据筛选提供高效排序训练方法。

quick_skim.title: DynaCF: Mitigating Shortcut Learning in Reward Models via Dynamic Counterfactual Sensitivity
quick_skim.abstract: Reward models trained from pairwise preferences often exploit superficial shortcut cues rather than learning true response quality. We propose DynaCF, a dynamic reweighting framework for mitigating shortcut learning in reward model training. Unlike static shortcut heuristics, DynaCF measures shortcut sensitivity online during optimization by applying semantics-preserving counterfactual perturbations and tracking the resulting margin shifts and preference flips under the current model. Samples with higher shortcut sensitivity are dynamically downweighted in the Bradley-Terry objective, encouraging the model to rely less on superficial patterns and more on task-relevant preference signals. Extensive experiments show that DynaCF consistently improves robustness in preference modeling.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: dynamic reweighting framework to mitigate shortcut learning in reward model training, applicable to reranker loss design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 动态重加权框架缓解奖励模型训练中的捷径学习，可应用于重排序器损失函数设计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 动态重加权框架缓解奖励模型训练中的捷径学习，可应用于重排序器损失函数设计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 动态重加权框架缓解奖励模型训练中的捷径学习，可应用于重排序器损失函数设计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DynaCF introduces a dynamic counterfactual sensitivity reweighting method that detects and downweights samples with high shortcut sensitivity during reward model training, improving reliance on task-relevant signals.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出DynaCF框架，用于缓解奖励模型训练中的捷径学习问题。现有奖励模型容易利用表面捷径线索而非真实偏好。DynaCF在优化过程中在线评估每个样本的捷径敏感性，通过施加语义保持的反事实扰动并观察边际变化，动态降低高敏感性样本在Bradley-Terry目标中的权重。实验表明该方法显著减少了捷径依赖，提升了偏好信号的学习质量。该工作为训练更鲁棒的排序模型（如重排序器）提供了实用的训练调整技术。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出DynaCF框架，用于缓解奖励模型训练中的捷径学习问题。现有奖励模型容易利用表面捷径线索而非真实偏好。DynaCF在优化过程中在线评估每个样本的捷径敏感性，通过施加语义保持的反事实扰动并观察边际变化，动态降低高敏感性样本在Bradley-Terry目标中的权重。实验表明该方法显著减少了捷径依赖，提升了偏好信号的学习质量。该工作为训练更鲁棒的排序模型（如重排序器）提供了实用的训练调整技术。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: loss function used in reranker training
